# Greenfield Projects

**IMPORTANT**: If the workspace contains no codebase for the target product (greenfield build), you will find the JanumiCode tooling workspace instead. In that case:
- Note that the target product has no existing codebase — set `codebaseFindings` to [] and note greenfield status in `analysisSummary`

# Critical Rules

- DO NOT modify any files. This is read-only investigation.
- DO NOT propose architecture decisions. That's the next phase's job.
- DO NOT ask questions. Produce findings only.
- Be thorough — read actual files, don't guess from names.

CDN (INT-CDN) will be used for static asset delivery.\n    *   **Security:** Malicious URL scanning (WF-3), strong password hashing, and protection against common web vulnerabilities will be paramount. `Audit Logging` (ENT-AUDITLOG) and `Centralized Logging & Monitoring` (INT-LOGGING-MONITORING) are crucial.\n    *   **Availability and Recoverability:** The architecture will consider redundancy and point-in-time recovery for the database. `Centralized Logging & Monitoring` (INT-LOGGING-MONITORING) and `Administrator Alerting` (INT-ADMIN-ALERTS) will support high availability.\n    *   **Usability:** The UI will be designed for minimal interaction for anonymous URL creation (UJ-1).\n    *   **Data Integrity and Compliance:** Transactions will ensure data consistency.       "name": "Unique Alias Generation",
      "description": "Ensures every new shortened URL is given a unique, non-colliding short code (alias) when one is not specified by the user.",
      "steps": [
        "A request to shorten a new URL is received.",
        "The system generates a random alphanumeric string of a predefined length.",
        "The system checks the database to ensure the generated string is not already in use as an alias.",
        "If the alias is unique, it is assigned to the new URL. If it already exists, the system loops back to generate a new one.",
        "The mapping of the unique alias to the long URL is saved to the database."
      ],
      "triggers": [
        "A user submitting a URL to be shortened without a custom alias."
      ],
      "actors": [
        "System"
      ],
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "WF-2",
      "businessDomainId": "DOM-ANALYTICS",
      "name": "Asynchronous Click Data Ingestion",
      "description": "Records click events quickly and reliably without slowing down the user redirection process.",
      "steps": [
        "A request for a short URL is received by the redirection endpoint.",
        "The system immediately looks up the long URL.",
        "A message containing click metadata (e.g., timestamp, user agent, IP address, short URL ID) is placed onto a high-speed message queue or buffer.",
        "The system issues the HTTP redirect to the user's browser.",
        "A separate, asynchronous worker process reads the click message from the queue.",
        "The worker process sanitizes and writes the click data to the main analytics database."
      ],
      "triggers": [
        "An End User clicking any valid, active shortened URL."
      ],
      "actors": [
        "System"
      ],
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "WF-3",
      "businessDomainId": "DOM-ADMIN",
      "name": "Automated Malicious URL Scanning",
      "description": "Proactively checks newly submitted URLs against known third-party security blocklists to identify and flag malicious content automatically.",
      "steps": [
        "A user submits a new URL to be shortened.",
        "After the short URL is created, the original long URL is added to a scanning queue.",
        "A background worker takes the URL from the queue.",
        "The worker makes an API call to a threat intelligence service (e.g., Google Safe Browsing API).",
        "If the service flags the URL as malicious, the system automatically disables the short URL and notifies an administrator.",
        "If the URL is safe, it is marked as 'scanned' and no action is taken."
      ],
      "triggers": [
        "Creation of any new short URL."
      ],
      "actors": [
        "System",
        "Third-Party Security Service"
